headline: Geopolitical Tensions Rise; AI Chip Market Sees Price Hikes, Policy Influence
summary: Markets reacted to renewed tariff threats and Middle East talks, with Treasury yields rising. The AI chip sector experienced pricing adjustments and ongoing policy influence, while memory markets showed signs of a structural upcycle.

bullets.lead: Trump threatens 100% tariffs on European nations over digital taxes.

segments.text: Former President Trump has threatened to impose a 100% tariff on European countries that implement a digital services tax, escalating trade tensions.

sources.title: Trump threatens 100% tariff on European countries that impose digital tax
Trump threatens 100% tariff on European nations over tech tax
Trump Threatens to Impose 100% Tariff on European Countries Over Tech Taxes

bullets.lead: Trump is actively promoting an Iran deal, despite low domestic approval.

segments.text: Trump is initiating a sales pitch for an Iran deal, which currently garners little support among Americans.

sources.title: Trump Kicks Off Sales Pitch for Iran Deal Few Americans Like

bullets.lead: China's Geely is set to ship its first Lotus EVs to Canada in July.

segments.text: Geely, a Chinese automaker, plans to ship its initial batch of Lotus electric vehicles to Canada next month, following a deal between Carney and Xi.

sources.title: Exclusive-China’s Geely to ship first Lotus EVs to Canada in July under Carney-Xi deal, ambassador says

bullets.lead: The US government has allowed some access to Anthropic's Mythos model.

segments.text: The Trump administration has permitted some access to Anthropic's Mythos model, a move that comes amidst broader government influence on AI model release protocols and intensifying competition between OpenAI and Anthropic.

sources.title: Trump administration allows some access to Anthropic’s Mythos

bullets.lead: Trump has requested OpenAI to stagger the release of its new AI model.

segments.text: Former President Trump has asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its latest AI model, indicating ongoing governmental interest in AI development timelines.

sources.title: Trump Asks OpenAI to Stagger Release of New Model, Google Pressures Publishers on AI Licensing — TITV [Video]

segments.text: Hanmi Semiconductor is positioning its FC Bonder 3.5 to capitalize on the growing demand for advanced packaging solutions in the semiconductor industry.

segments.text: Micron's revenue has surged ninefold in a year, reflecting the explosive demand for AI-driven memory solutions and signaling an AI-driven memory supercycle expected to persist beyond 2030.

x_themes.summary: The US government is lifting export blocks on Anthropic's Mythos 5 model, while also influencing model release protocols. Competition between OpenAI and Anthropic is intensifying, with Chinese models like DeepSeek gaining traction due to lower costs, prompting calls for stronger US authentication and innovation.

TSMC detailed its technology roadmap, including 6T SRAM breakthroughs, 98% yield for CoWoS 5.5x reticle size, and aggressive N2/A16 capacity expansion (70% CAGR 2026-2028). AI and HPC chips are projected to account for 55% of the semiconductor market by 2030, with TSMC manufacturing Qualcomm's new data center CPUs and OpenAI's custom AI inference chips.

The memory market is in a structural upcycle with rising DRAM and HBM prices, expected to persist beyond 2030 due to AI-driven demand. Micron's FYQ3 EPS guidance aligns with expectations, with traditional DRAM prices rising ~45% and HBM prices projected to be 80% higher by end-CY2027.

The photonics and optical interconnect markets are in an early supercycle, with companies like AAOI ramping 800G/1.6T transceiver capacity and SiPh/Optical Interconnect markets projected to grow at 20-30% CAGR through 2030. InP fabs and 6-inch capacity are critical for this expansion.

NVIDIA's NVFP4 quantization significantly reduces the HBM footprint for AI models, cutting GLM-5.2 model-weight memory by ~69% compared to non-quantized versions. This efficiency allows running large models on fewer Blackwell-class GPUs, reducing serving costs without degrading output quality.

OpenAI unveiled its new GPT-5.6 Sol model, claiming state-of-the-art performance on Terminal-Bench 2.1 and outperforming Anthropic's Mythos 5 and Fable 5 in coding workflows. Initial access is limited to White House-approved partners, with broader availability planned.

The upstream PCB supply chain is experiencing a supercycle with critical friction concentrated in glass fabric, copper foil, and Copper Clad Laminate. Supply deficits for glass fabric are noted as substantially more severe than for copper foil, alongside relentless capacity constraints in the IC substrate sector.

Compute demand is becoming a significant bottleneck for AI, with H100 one-year lease rates rebounding 40% and Anthropic securing over 220k NVIDIA GPUs and 300MW from SpaceX Colossus 1 to meet incremental capacity needs.
Datacenters are increasingly building behind-the-meter power generation assets due to perceived weaknesses in the US grid, despite the higher costs and complexity involved.

The space sector is facing headwinds from new IPOs causing rotations, increased competition from SpaceX impacting smaller players like ASTS, broader macro impacts from hawkish Fed rates, and incidents like the Blue Origin explosion.
Rumors of an 800VDC architecture delay were pushed back, with Episil Technologies confirming Nvidia's goal to introduce the 800VDC architecture in 2027 remains on schedule.

x_themes.topic: Qualcomm's High Bandwidth Compute for AI
x_themes.summary: Qualcomm plans to integrate its High Bandwidth Compute architecture, which vertically stacks memory and compute, into smartphones, PCs, and cars. This aims to improve data flow and enable more local AI models and always-on agents on mobile devices.

Morgan Stanley maintains its forecast for the Federal Reserve to hold interest rates through year-end, with incoming data since the June FOMC meeting making them "marginally more comfortable" with a no-hike baseline.

The economic value of AI models automating jobs is complex; while the marginal contribution of an additional AI instance might be low due to competition, the total contribution to GDP will be significantly higher due to widespread adoption and parallel operation.
